channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: The Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in a new memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal agencies must develop plans to eliminate employee positions, according to a memo distributed by President Donald Trump 's administration that sets in motion what could become a sweeping realignment of American government.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hamas will turn over bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for release of hundreds of prisoners
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas was set to hand over the bodies of four hostages late Wednesday night in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, just days before the first phase of the ceasefire between the warring parties was to expire.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The US Christian population has declined for years. A new survey shows that drop leveling off
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans who identify as Christian has declined steadily for years, but that drop shows signs of slowing, according to a new survey Wednesday from the Pew Research Center. The Religious Landscape Study finds 62% of U.S. adults call themselves Christians.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Oscars producers promise fresh energy, big stars and surprises at the show
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “I’m Just Ken” is a hard act to follow. Last year’s Oscars was by all accounts a successful show, with memorable performances and unscripted moments (thanks in part to Emma Stone’s dress), and a winning movie that most people had seen.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, KRON4

item.title: Computer simulations show nightmare Atlantic current shutdown less likely this century
item.description: A new study reassures people that the nightmare disaster movie scenario of Atlantic Ocean currents collapsing, with weather running amok putting Europe in a deep freeze, looks unlikely this century thanks to a rescue from around Antarctica.
item.dc:creator: By Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, KRON4

item.title: Idaho prosecutors want to block Bryan Kohberger from arguing an 'alternative perpetrator' left blood at scene
item.description: Idaho prosecutors are asking a Boise judge to block Bryan Kohberger's defense from arguing an "alternative perpetrator" in their latest court filings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unvaccinated child dies in measles outbreak that infected over 120 people
item.description: A person who was hospitalized with measles has died from measles in West Texas, the first death in an outbreak that began late last month.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prominent Colorado developer assassinated at luxury Belize home: report
item.description: A real estate developer from Denver, Colorado was fatally shot in Belize and police say it could have been the result of a professional hit by a disgruntled business associate, according to local reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Not known for political coverage, Wired takes a leading role in tracking Elon Musk's team
item.description: The tech outlet Wired set up a new politics team in time for the presidential election — but its most impactful work is coming after the election is over.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diana Taurasi of the Phoenix Mercury retires after 20 WNBA seasons, 3 titles and 6 Olympic golds
item.description: Diana Taurasi is retiring after 20 seasons, ending one of the greatest careers in women's basketball history.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL and DOUG FEINBERG AP Basketball Writers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas leads nation in housing ICE detainees
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facilities in Texas are holding the most immigrants, according to new data.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis sits upright in an armchair as prayers for his recovery continue
item.description: Doctors have said he isn't out of danger, but hasn't had any further respiratory crises since Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams claims prosecutorial misconduct, asking judge to dismiss criminal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams is asking a federal judge to toss out the corruption case against him, alleging prosecutorial misconduct, even as the Justice Department seeks dismissal of the charges on the Democrat's behalf.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American Airlines jet pulls out of DC landing to avoid collision
item.description: An American Airlines jet avoided a collision with another plane while preparing to land at Reagan National Airport in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crucial Hamptons bridge closes indefinitely ahead of spring season
item.description: Officials in the state of New York are trying to figure out if work needs to be done on the Ponquogue Bridge after deteriorating concrete was found during a routine inspection last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University suspends fraternity accused of ‘inhumane’ hazing that left pledge in fear
item.description: A fraternity at Miami University in Ohio has been suspended after a student complained of "inhumane" hazing and undergoing abuse, according to a hazing incident report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Police video and 911 call play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's murder
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A harrowing 911 call, disturbing police videos and other crime scene evidence are expected to play a key role as the murder and hate crime trial continues Wednesday for an Illinois landlord accused of killing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: He's had three 'last meals' on Oklahoma's death row. The Supreme Court has now tossed his conviction
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Richard Glossip has spent 27 years behind bars, most of it on Oklahoma's death row, coming close enough to execution that he has had nine separate execution dates and been fed three “last meals.” On Tuesday, Glossip, now 62, won a new trial in a stunning U.S.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: CARFAX expert tells you what to know before purchasing a car
item.description: Carfax's Editor-in-Chief Patrick Olsen reports that the agency monitors vehicle prices across seven categories.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by midday Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Roberta Flack, Grammy-winning ‘Killing Me Softly’ singer with an intimate style, dies at 88
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Roberta Flack, the Grammy-winning singer and pianist whose intimate vocal and musical style made her one of the top recordings artists of the 1970s and an influential performer long after, died Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
